licence: torchreid is MIT; the OSNet-AIN pretrained weights' terms are not yet reviewed — check before redistribution

toolchain.note: QAT uses horizon_plugin_pytorch inside the OE container; the .hbm is produced by hbdk4.compiler convert+compile, not hb_compile.

cosine.note: COSINE IS NOT THE GATE FOR THIS MODEL. A degraded ReID tower still emits well-formed unit vectors: int8 PTQ here holds a cosine that does not scream 'broken' while Rank-1 falls to 51%. The real acceptance is task_metric below. Layer A for a QAT model compares the quantized graph to the FP32 teacher (there is no separate quantized ONNX).

task_metric.name: Market-1501 single-query Rank-1 / mAP, cross-domain (weights from MSMT17, scored on Market-1501)
task_metric.protocol: 100-identity subset, fixed seed; same-camera same-id excluded; distractors/junk dropped. Board and host run the identical subset via market.py --bundle.

measured.note: mAP of the QAT build is on par with or slightly above the float baseline on this subset. The Rank-1 gap of 0.2 pt is within subset noise.
From the original M10 adaptation on S100P (HBRT 4.7.5), the SHIPPED QAT build. Latency from hrt_model_exec on a quiet board.
measured.fake_quant_prepare: one pass, no operator-coverage gaps — a pure CNN, unlike attention stacks

qat_recipe.why: int8 PTQ is unusable on this network; QAT self-distillation is the only build that works.
qat_recipe.supervision: FP32 teacher, no identity labels — loss = 1 - cos(student, teacher). A function-matching problem, not identity learning.
qat_recipe.loss_choice: cosine, not L2: the embedding is L2-normalized before use, so its magnitude is not in the contract.
qat_recipe.data: ~11k unlabelled COCO + KITTI person crops (crops.py). Market-1501 is never trained on.
qat_recipe.schedule: 6-8 epochs, ~20 min on one GPU. cosine-vs-FP32 climbs 0.40 -> 0.978.
qat_recipe.offline_fidelity: The QAT (fake-quant) graph reproduces the board's failure in torch (cosine 0.5049 torch vs 0.475 board), so tuning iterates in seconds against the number the board will show.

rejected_builds.name: int8 PTQ, osnet_ain_x1_0 (config.yaml)
rejected_builds.why: Compiles clean, returns plausible unit vectors, ranks at 51% vs float 85%. This is the whole reason QAT exists. Reproduce with calib.py + config.yaml.

rejected_builds.name: int8 PTQ, plain-BN osnet_x1_0
rejected_builds.why: Counter-intuitive: dropping InstanceNorm makes quantisation FAR worse. IN is a quantisation asset here, not a liability.

rejected_builds.name: int8 PTQ, osnet_ibn_x1_0
rejected_builds.why: Also collapses. Neither calibration method (modelwise/max) nor set size (64->400 crops; 400 was slightly worse) rescued any int8 variant.

rejected_builds.name: different backbone (ResNet50) to dodge the problem
rejected_builds.why: ResNet50's float cross-domain Rank-1 (46.3) is far below OSNet-AIN's (70.1 full test set). Changing backbone loses more than quantisation ever did.

rejected_builds.name: convert_fx to lower the QAT graph in PyTorch
rejected_builds.why: Dead end: quantized InstanceNorm and AdaptiveAvgPool2d both reduce over dim=(2,3), which the plugin's quantized mean has no path for. Use the vendor HBIR convert flow instead (deploy.py).

layer_C_hbm_vs_fp32.verdict: PASS on the task metric — Rank-1 84.62 vs 84.84 float
layer_C_hbm_vs_fp32.note: Bit-identity (layer B) is not claimed: the converted graph carries float CPU ops, like the ppocr rec build. Task equivalence (Rank-1) is the right layer-B/C check for a ReID embedding.

board_verification.note: These are the shipped-build numbers from the original adaptation. A from-scratch rebuild through this recipe has not been re-verified in this repo; the calibration crop set and QAT seed reproduce the method, not necessarily the exact binary.
